subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand what markets are and why they exist
Identify different types of markets in the locality
Learn about goods, services, and price variations
Observe buyer-seller interactions and trade practices
Develop observation, comparison, and basic analytical skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Markets
topics_covered: Purpose of markets in daily life
teaching_strategy: Show pictures of local markets and shops
Ask students about markets they visit
student_activities: Share names of markets they know
Discuss what they buy or see in markets

lesson_plan.title: Types of Markets
topics_covered: Local markets, weekly haats, and supermarkets
Differences between small shops and large stores
teaching_strategy: Use charts or slides showing different market types
Compare pictures of traditional and modern markets
student_activities: Identify different types of markets around them
Discuss which type they prefer and why

lesson_plan.title: Goods and Services
teaching_strategy: Ask students to sort items into goods and services
student_activities: Create a small list of goods and services seen in a market

lesson_plan.title: Price and Bargaining
topics_covered: How prices vary in different shops
teaching_strategy: Show examples of price tags and price differences
Role-play a small buyer-seller scenario in class
student_activities: Simulate buying an item and bargaining
Compare prices of items from different shops

lesson_plan.title: Trade and Exchange
topics_covered: How trade helps people get what they need
teaching_strategy: Storytelling about barter and early trade
student_activities: Simulate a simple barter activity in class
Identify what could be exchanged in real-life markets

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Fun Quiz
topics_covered: Recap of market types, goods, services, and trade
Simple cause-effect questions about market activities
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures and multiple choice
Class discussion relating markets to daily life
student_activities: Draw or share one thing they learned about markets

teaching_aids: Pictures of local markets, shops, and products
Simple props for trade and barter demonstration
Drawing sheets and colors for activities
teacher_tip: Use real-life examples and encourage students to relate markets to their daily shopping experiences. Keep activities interactive with role-play and drawing tasks.
